steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: On the left side of the blank: “During the American Civil War, thousands of women joined the war effort as nurses” is an independent clause. On the right side: “Among them was African American nurse Ann Bradford Stokes, who was later commended for her dedication to serving the medical needs of countless soldiers” is an independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the sentence boundary rule
steps.content: Two independent clauses require a sentence boundary (period, semicolon, or comma + coordinating conjunction); choice C correctly uses a period. Choices A, B, and D violate this requirement—A creates a comma splice, B creates a run-on, and D uses malformed punctuation. That matches choice C — C is correct.
